calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-28-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Don Lippenberger for Larry Kwong (Petitioner/Administrator).

Mr. Lippenberger stated that they have two waivers of the accounting, that one waiver was filed on August 17, 2018, that Ms. Kwong was here at the last hearing and waived it, and that there were too many papers to list in the area allotted on the notice of hearing. The Court noted that Counsel could have done a second notice of hearing. Mr. Lippenberger stated that they needed an Administrator in place in order to file a claim in the Stanley Kwong estate, that they were up against a four month deadline, that they didn't have a will at the time and did not know that there was a will, and that while they were in the process of looking for documents regarding the claim, they found Harry's will. Mr. Lippenberger also complained about the Court's process and some of the issues raised by the Probate Examiner, and stated that this is pretty straight forward, and the Court has made things difficult. The Court stated that this has not been straight forward, that it has been a moving target, that a large part of it has to do with Counsel not following the Rules of Court rules that have been set by the Legislature and noted that the will was presented late in the case. Mr. Lippenberger stated that the Probate Examiner raised the issue of the waiver having been signed by Ms. Kwong as a Beneficiary, not as a Trustee and that he has not responded to the Probate Examiner's notes. The Fourth and Final Report of Administrator, etc. is under submission. Mr. Lippenberger was told that in the future he is not to use an attachment as the notice.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-11-2019 Continued To Jan-22-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Don Lippenberger for Larry Kwong (Petitioner).
Phil Foster for and with Jeanne Kwong (Beneficiary).

Mr. Lippenberger stated that he needs clarification of the tentative ruling. The Court refers Mr. Lippenberger to Local Rule 7.53. Mr. Lippenberger stated that the waiver of accounting was filed on August 17, 2018. Mr. Foster stated that fact of waiver is true, that Jeanne has been served with all papers and that Mr. Lippenberger filed her waiver. Ms. Kwong confirmed that she waives the accounting.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-17-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Don Lippenberger for Larry Kwong (Administrator).

Mr. Lippenberger stated that he filed an amended petition because the Court required it, that there were no assets in the estate until they received a judgment, that the Trustee of the Trust disclaimed her interest and that she is also the Beneficiary. The Court informed Counsel that he needs to file a new petition with the will attached, that he needs to explain what happened to the $100,000.00, that distribution needs to be made to the trust and that he needs to make a request to appoint a Trustee. The Petition for Final Distribution is denied.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
